next_step_text: July 20 - follow up Procurement Strategy Update Meeting Overview Edward Dlugosz, George Andrus, and Lee Teney (the two US decision makers) discussed the status of their procurement initiative at Mizuho, which has been paused due to the need for a global procurement framework. Lee expressed gratitude for the team's work and acknowledged the six-month delay. Edward offered to provide information on

recent_activities_14d.subject: [Clari - Email Received] RE: [Exclusive Invitation (Lee & George)] Horizon US 2026: Zycus’ Premier Procurement Conferenc
[Clari - Email Received] RE: [Exclusive Invitation (Lee & George)] Horizon US 2026: Zycus’ Premier Procurement Conferenc

our_open_deliverables.text: Send Lee, George, Matt and Alex a summary on Zycus's Japanese-market penetration and native Japanese deployments

buyer_dependent_items.text: Share the expected restart date/decision point once the global procurement framework direction is set

competitive_position.summary: Recency-weighted read: this is a frozen three-finalist bake-off with no buyer-endorsed vendor. GEP Worldwide is the single strongest current threat, favoured by the buyer's scale/US-bank-reference concern (29 Dec 2025), with a third undisclosed finalist still in. The most live alternative is actually the status quo, keeping invoicing in SAP, which the buyer explicitly asked to be costed on 9 Dec 2025 and which the 6-month pause effectively defaults to. Coupa, SAP Ariba, iValua, JAGGAER, Oracle, Sirion Labs and DocuSign appear in the Competitors field but are earlier-stage or faded with no fresh 2026 buyer evidence; per living memory they are retained, not re-ranked, on this low-new-evidence run.

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.quote: Mizuho questioned Zycus's ability to keep pace with larger competitors and cited the lack of a major US-bank deployment ('what are our peers doing').
competitors.how_we_win: Merlin AP Smartdesk plus single-codebase integration and APAC banking proof (MUFG, Maybank) against GEP's multi-acquisition scale.

competitors.name: Status quo (keep invoicing in SAP)
competitors.quote: We need to know what it looks like if we maintain everything in SAP to give us the true color of what our options are.
competitors.how_we_win: Quantify manual AP touch-cost and reporting gaps that persist in SAP versus Zycus touchless processing and Merlin analytics.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: Listed in Competitors__c; Ariba tool is part of the current invoice-intake stack.
competitors.source: SF Competitors__c + Avoma 9 Dec 2025 (Ariba tool referenced)
competitors.how_we_win: Displace fragmented Ariba/SAP intake with unified Zycus S2P and Merlin AP automation.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Listed in Competitors__c; no fresh 2026 buyer evidence.
competitors.how_we_win: Deeper AP-agent and compliance depth; carried forward, not re-ranked.

competitors.name: iValua
competitors.quote: CRM Competition: Ariba, Coupa, Ivalua, Jaggaer.
competitors.how_we_win: Carried forward from prior read; no live signal.

competitors.name: JAGGAER
competitors.quote: CRM Competition: Ariba, Coupa, Ivalua, Jaggaer.
competitors.how_we_win: Carried forward from prior read; no live signal.

competitors.name: Sirion Labs
competitors.how_we_win: Point CLM alternative; iContract counters if contracting resurfaces.

champion_strength.source: OpportunityContactRole (Matthew Brzoza, Amit Saraff as Influencers) + absence of advocacy across calls 8 Sep 2025-22 Jun 2026
champion_strength.summary: No active champion. Matthew Brzoza and Amit Saraff are the developing-champion candidates but neither advocates on calls; George Andrus and Lee Tenny are warm but run a fair, transactional evaluation. In a tight three-way bake-off now frozen for six months, the absence of an internal seller is the deal's core structural weakness.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: David Wong (CFO), Takashi Minowa (Deputy CFO), Scott Buckleton (Exec Sponsor), Lee Tenny
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: Budget authority is named but not directly engaged by Zycus. Tavannie Perez stated on 9 Dec 2025 that a decision of this size 'wouldn't come from me. That is something David Wong would have to approve because that is a huge change in the process', naming David Wong (Managing Director & CFO) as the approver for any material process change. Takashi Minowa (Deputy Chief Financial Officer, Managing Director) sits in the same finance chain as an Influencer contact role. On the US side Lee Tenny (Head of Procurement and Third Party Risk Management) is the functional sponsor who relayed the management pause directive on 22 Jun 2026, and Scott Buckleton (VP Procurement) is the primary Executive Sponsor of record. The gap is access: neither David Wong nor Takashi Minowa has appeared on a working call, so the true budget owner is mapped by title but not sponsored. Securing a direct finance-level conversation ahead of restart is the power move.

recommended_moves: Send the Zycus Japan-market and native-Japanese-deployment summary to Lee, George, Matt and Alex.
Use the July 20 update meeting to co-build a quantified AP savings case with Yolanda Ferrigno.
Enlist Matthew Brzoza or Amit Saraff to carry the internal restart business case for Zycus.
